title: A sugar-based supplement (D-mannose) taken with water for preventing Urinary Tract Infections (UTIs)
reason: Blocks bacteria from sticking to the bladder wall — counts as a pharmacological action under both the Manual and the 2025 EU Court ruling.

title: A light-activated drug used on a sample of blood cells outside the body, which are then re-injected to treat a type of blood cancer
reason: The drug does the cell-killing work by pharmacological, immunological and metabolic action once UV-activated; the equipment is a separate

title: Bone-filler paste containing animal tissue and animal growth factors to stimulate bone growth via a scaffolding effect
reason: The paste is a physical scaffold for bone growth; the stimulating nature of the animal growth factors is merely ancillary to the physical matrix.

title: Antimicrobial silver-infused clothing to prevent atopic dermatitis
reason: Silver is a medicinal substance, presented as having anti-microbial properties. The combination of clothes with a medicinal substance might not be qualified as a medical device.

title: Bacteria-detection test kit for checking infection risk in donated blood
reason: The system is providing information regarding the safety of blood donation and therefore should be qualified as an in vitro diagnostic medical device.

title: Dual action cream containing mint/chilly extract (Menthol/Capsaicin) to relieve muscle and joint pain
reason: Both substances, menthol and capsaicin, have a recognized pharmacological action in pain management.

title: App that lets sexual partners share STI results and sends automatic alerts to those at risk
reason: The risk calculation is based on indirect criteria and not on physiological parameters. The software does not perform an action on data other than communication.

title: A menstrual tampon
reason: Menstruation is not considered a medical condition despite the tampon requiring insertion in the vagina.

title: Disposable paper covers for exam tables to ensure hygiene and prevent the transmission of infectious agents
reason: They are intended to be used for a specific medical purpose and act via a physical barrier. This qualification is not dependent on the cover's composition/chosen materials.

title: Breath-test device measuring nitric oxide for detection of respiratory disease
reason: Exhaled breath becomes a 'specimen' once outside the body; analyzing it for disease-related information is diagnostic.

title: Spray that gives antimicrobial properties to textiles/surfaces to prevent the spread of human infectious diseases
reason: This product does not act on individual patients, rather, it imparts antifungal, antimicrobial and antiviral properties onto textiles.

title: Light-activated eye drops (riboflavin) used to stiffen the cornea and halt disease progression.
reason: Riboflavin results in an alteration of the normal chemical process of cross-linking of collagen via a metabolic action.

title: Dental liquid/gel with aluminium chloride to stop gum bleeding
reason: Stops bleeding through precipitation of albumins which in turn block the capillaries. The mode of action is physical barrier and not pharmacological.

title: Hand disinfectant gel
reason: Disinfects hands generally; not a device and not an accessory to one.

title: Single-use sterile brushes/sponges for pre-surgery hand washing
reason: Doesn't meet the device definition even if containing disinfectants.

title: Teeth-whitening gel or strips
reason: Tooth discoloration isn't a disease, so whitening doesn't 'treat' anything in the regulatory sense — even with claims about reducing bad breath or cavities, there's no proven medical purpose.

title: Haemostatic pencil used to seal cuts and stop the bleeding with a combination of a waxy binder and astringent aluminum salts
reason: The waxy layer plus the induced precipitation of proteins lead to the physical blockage of the damaged blood vessels.

title: A weight-loss pill that inhibits a digestive enzyme
reason: Inhibits an enzyme, thus altering a metabolic process.

title: Tooth-whiteners acting via microabrasion
reason: The removal of dental stains to improve the appearance of teeth does not represent a medical purpose.

title: Padded bag for transporting injured patients during rescue operations
reason: The product is intended to protect the patient mechanically, as well as thermally, during the salvage. This corresponds to the medical purpose of alleviation of, or compensation for, an injury or disability.

title: Plexiglas box to protect medical staff from infection during intubation of a patient
reason: It's designed to protect the healthcare worker, not the patient — a device's purpose has to be for the patient's benefit. Products that are worn by an individual healthcare worker are regulated under the regulations covering personal protective equipment; however, these regulations do not cover products which are not worn by an individual.

title: Radiation shields to protect healthcare professionals while treating patients
reason: They protect staff in the room from radiation exposure, not the patient — and radiation exposure itself isn't a disease although it may cause disease.

title: Rugby helmet to protect players from brain injury
reason: The helmet does not prevent, treat or alleviate a disease nor does it treat or alleviate an injury.

title: Single-use examination gloves coated with a bactericide substance (PHMB) to prevent contamination
reason: Two issues need to be considered: a) is the substance (PHMB), if used separately a medicinal product; b) is the substance liable to act on the human body with action ancillary to that of the devices? Depending on the mode of action of the substance, the gloves are devices incorporating a medicinal substance.

title: Capsule/stirrer system that mixes generates a foam used in treatment of varicose veins
reason: The foam, which is considered a medicinal product, is going to be introduced afterwards in the body by a syringe which is not part of the system. Neither the system itself nor the capsule or the stirrer fulfill the definition of a medical device.

title: Air purifiers and air decontamination products intended to remove allergens or bacteria (e.g. in operating theaters)
reason: These products are intended to 'control the environment'. They do not act directly on an individual patient and there is no direct contact with an individual patient.

title: Rectal laxative medicine packaged in a single-dose applicator tube for rectal insertion
reason: Because the product forms a single integral medicinal product, which is intended exclusively for use in the given combination and which is not reusable, the medical device part (the packaging) must comply with the requirements of the MDR but only as part of the marketing authorisation dossier of the integral medicinal product.

title: Software app helping clinicians review a patients' ECG history to support diagnosis and treatment
reason: The app uses signal data from an external source and processes it to an ECG waveform thereby performing an action on data other than just storage, for the medical benefit of individual patients.

title: App for messaging/notes between a birthing patient and her care team
reason: Only stores and allows for searching notes and communication — no analysis or processing for medical benefit.

title: App showing illustrated anatomy of the human body
reason: Purely educational reference material; it is not used directly for the medical benefit of the individual patients.

title: App for managing pictures of skin moles for showing them to a doctor
reason: Just stores images for the user to show a doctor later — no analysis performed by the app itself.

title: App that analyzes photos of skin moles and estimates melanoma risk
reason: Uses image-processing/algorithms to actually assess the moles — that's an action on data beyond simple storage, for the patient's benefit.

title: A device to facilitate conception using oral temperature and cycle tracking to predict fertile days
reason: Intended purpose is to facilitate conception, which is a recognized medical purpose under the device definition.

title: A device to support dual conception/contraception goals using oral temperature and cycle tracking to predict fertile days
reason: Unwanted pregnancy is a case of human vulnerability. All devices used for contraception or the prevention of the transmission of sexually transmitted diseases are in Class IIb.

title: Mobile app for natural family planning combining calendar, temperature, and cervical-mucus data entered by the user
reason: Calculating a fertility window for contraception purposes is a medical purpose, regardless of it being pure software with user-entered data. Unwanted pregnancy is considered a case of human vulnerability.

title: Software guiding healthcare professionals in optimising patient medicinal therapy (contraindications, interactions, dosing)
reason: The software is intended to be used for the purpose of prevention, monitoring, treatment or alleviation of a disease and should therefore be qualified as a medical device.

title: Point-of-care medical calculators based on formulas documented in the medical literature
reason: The calculation is for the benefit of individual patients, it provides information which is used to take decisions with diagnosis or therapeutic purposes, and it acts of the data beyond simple search, storage and communication. Therefore it meets the definition of medical device software.

title: Bed-wetting alarm as a therapy for nocturnal incontinence
reason: Treats a recognized medical condition by conditioning the user to wake before wetting.

title: Sweat-stimulating (via drug) and analytical system (chloride test) for diagnosing cystic fibrosis
reason: The drug is acting in an ancillary manner by stimulating glands to ensure collection of sweat is more effective. But the primary purpose is sweat collection and analysis. The system is directly applied to the human body for the purposes of obtaining a sample and therefore is not considered to be an IVD, but rather a medical device.

title: A hydrogel containing a yeast-derived sugar (beta-glucan) to be applied as a primary wound dressing.
reason: The primary action is moisturizing/healing the wound (device-like), but the beta-glucan additionally stimulates immune cells in a way that, on its own, would count as medicinal.

title: Vibration-sensor mat for babies that detects risky inactivity
reason: Intended to help prevent a life-threatening medical event (apnoea/SIDS), and is an active, electrically-powered medical device.

title: Hydrocolloid plaster for rapid blister healing
reason: Works on the wound's microenvironment (moisture/warmth) rather than acting only as a passive barrier. The active/rapid healing claim pushes it to a higher class than a basic plaster.
